headline: US government export-control action shut down Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 within days of release, dominating the quarter.
body: A US government export-control directive required Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers after roughly three days of availability, with commentary citing jailbreak and access concerns and EU jurisdiction effects. In parallel, an inference-speed race broke out: Google's DiffusionGemma applies diffusion techniques to text generation to clear 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs, while Xiaomi and TileRT's trillion-parameter MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed reaches similar throughput via FP4 quantization and speculative decoding. On the tooling side, vibe coding broke out around Google Antigravity's agent-first surfaces and the Gemma-powered AIventure project, and the Model Context Protocol entered as a new technique alongside JSON-RPC and client-server communication.

cited_items.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
